description: Scripted multi-turn conversations against chains.wizard_chat_chain.wizard_chat. Each case replays a fixed sequence of user turns (optionally starting from a seeded partial_config to shortcut earlier fields) and every turn is checked with eval/wizard_checks.py's deterministic invariants -- chip/topic alignment, chip schema shape, ready_to_generate honesty, and no-inline-JSON-leak. This is a state-machine correctness eval, not a quality/preference eval -- see docs/eval-set.md Section 1 (Anya Wizard Chatbot) for the manual-QA equivalents this automates.

conversations.note: Reproduces the exact 2026-07-18 production bug: user asks Anya to recommend a budget, then asks for a cost breakdown -- the reply both times is about BUDGET, but the fallback chip-backfill logic was showing PACE chips underneath because config_patch.budget got set prematurely on the recommendation turn. Regression-checked by check_chip_topic_alignment.
turns: you tell me, i have no idea
my wife and 3 yr old son
can you give me a breakup of the expenses?

conversations.note: Full 6-field walk from scratch, checking structural chip validity at each step and that ready_to_generate is only ever true once every required field is actually present (ANYA-W-011/016 equivalents).
Direct ANYA-W-022 equivalent: once every field but pace is known, the very next reply must offer pace chips, not stale chips from an earlier field.
